AngleGap: iMocha Python tests category page — lists 6 Python tests (Python Foundations for AI, Python 3 Basic/Medium/High, Python Coding Basic/High) with 13k–17k candidate counts. 'Python Foundations for AI' shows no candidates/orgs counts (new/unfilled). No guidance on which Python test depth to use for Data Analyst vs. Data Scientist vs. AI/ML Engineer vs. Backend Developer hiring, no sample questions.

DistinctAngle: Makes the most-cited benchmark tables publicly accessible without gating — directly solving the friction this all-gated resource directory creates for HR professionals who want to reference data without a demo request.

AngleGap: iMocha skills-based hiring platform page — describes screening, AI proctoring, smart candidate matching, time-to-hire reduction, quality-of-hire improvement. References '10,000+ skills assessments' and 'WCAG 2.1 Compliant' but no specific benchmark data on quality-of-hire improvement rates, no comparison of skills-based vs. resume-based hiring outcomes, no sample assessment or scorecard shown publicly. Asset: Skills-Based Hiring vs. Resume-Based Hiring: A Data-Driven Comparison of Quality-of-Hire, Time-to-Hire, and Attrition Outcomes (With a Free Hiring Scorecard)
